channel.title: EMERGENZ Biosecurity Intelligence Dashboard
channel.description: High-severity biosecurity signals and recent authority/media coverage. Generated 2026-06-08T20:27:03.304Z.

item.title: [ACTION] Ebola disease — Bundibugyo virus, Democratic Republic of the Congo
item.description: WHO has declared the Bundibugyo ebolavirus outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo, with cross-border spread to Uganda. Per the WHO emergency situation page (data as of 31 May 2026 for the DRC and 1 June 2026 for Uganda), the DRC has reported 321 confirmed cases, 48 confirmed deaths (CFR 15%), and 116 suspected cases, and Uganda has reported 9 confirmed cases (1 death) — 330 confirmed cases and 49 deaths in total. Confirmed cases have risen sharply since the late-May WHO DON605 snapshot (125 confirmed) as laboratory testing reclassified earlier suspected cases, so the suspected count has correspondingly fallen from about 906 to 116. The regional response has escalated: Uganda border health measures, a contested US-supported quarantine/care arrangement in Kenya, a Brazilian investigation of possible imported cases, and accelerated multi-candidate vaccine efforts. WHO updates these figures daily from DRC government (COUSP-DRC) situation reports and they are subject to retrospective revision — verify the latest WHO situation report before operational use.

item.title: [CONCERN] Andes virus hantavirus cluster — MV Hondius travel-linked
item.description: Andes hantavirus cluster linked to a South America–Antarctica expedition cruise aboard MV Hondius. 13 reported cases (11 confirmed, 2 probable) and 3 deaths across multiple countries, with multinational case-finding and post-exposure monitoring continuing.

item.title: [CONCERN] Measles — United States
item.description: CDC confirmed 1,983 measles cases across 40 jurisdictions in 2026 (as of May 28), representing 30 outbreaks with 93% of cases outbreak-associated. No deaths reported in 2026. U.S. kindergartener MMR coverage has declined to 92.5% (from 95.2% in 2019–2020), leaving approximately 286,000 kindergarteners unvaccinated — the structural driver of sustained outbreak activity.

item.title: [CONCERN] Mpox — clade I, Africa & travel-linked importations
item.description: Mpox clade I and travel-linked importation monitoring remain operationally significant for clinical recognition, specimen routing, and laboratory clade differentiation. Verify current country-level trends and any importation details against specific Africa CDC, WHO, or CDC updates before publication.

item.title: [CONCERN] Cholera — sub-Saharan Africa, including Republic of Congo
item.description: Africa CDC reported 40,707 cholera cases continent-wide from January 1 through May 14, 2026 — a 65% year-over-year decline compared to the same period in 2025. Despite the overall volume decrease, case fatality ratios remain elevated in conflict-affected and low-healthcare-access settings. Sudan, Ethiopia, Somalia, Mozambique, Malawi, and Zimbabwe continue reporting active transmission.

item.title: [CONCERN] Chikungunya — global outbreak activity
item.description: Chikungunya surveillance remains active across parts of the Americas and other mosquito-active regions. Verify current country burden and case totals against specific PAHO or WHO updates before publication.

item.title: [CONCERN] Yellow fever — Colombia (Tolima) & the Americas
item.description: PAHO reports sustained yellow fever transmission across parts of South America, with cases expanding beyond traditional Amazonian risk zones. The department of Tolima, Colombia — where human cases had not been seen in over a century — is the current epicenter and concentrates the highest case and death counts in the country. Transmission is driven overwhelmingly by unvaccinated individuals. Verify current national figures against PAHO and Colombia's Instituto Nacional de Salud (INS) before publication; counts are revised as cases are laboratory-confirmed.

item.title: Google News: Measles cases in Buckingham County keep climbing as health officials identify two public exposure sites - Charlottesville Tomorrow
item.description: Measles cases in Buckingham County keep climbing as health officials identify two public exposure sites Charlottesville Tomorrow

item.title: STAT News: Health workers at the epicenter of Congo’s Ebola outbreak labor with little pay or rest
item.description: Health workers at the epicenter of Congo's Ebola outbreak are laboring with little pay or rest.

item.title: STAT News: STAT+: AstraZeneca’s GLP-1 pill shows promise in obesity, diabetes trials
item.description: AstraZeneca’s GLP-1 pill showed promise in mid-stage obesity and diabetes studies, but it may still be too early to determine how it stacks up against other oral treatments.

item.title: Africa CDC: European Union Announces €11.5 million Euros to support Africa CDC’s response to Ebola Outbreak
item.description: Addis Ababa, Ethiopia | 8th June 2026: The Africa Centres for Disease Control and Prevention (Africa CDC) today received €11.5 million renewed commitment from the European Union Commission (EU) to support a stronger preparedness and coordinated response to the Ebola...

item.title: Google News: Celebrating a wedding amid the Ebola outbreak: No kisses or close contact, but love lives here - Los Angeles Times
item.description: Celebrating a wedding amid the Ebola outbreak: No kisses or close contact, but love lives here Los Angeles Times
